content_gaps: The 'meaning' or symbolism of choosing a darkened, aged finish over a bright, polished one. Most pages focus entirely on the chemical process.
The range of colors achievable through oxidation (blues, purples, yellows) is mentioned briefly but not explored in depth.
DIY/at-home oxidation methods (like using boiled eggs) are mentioned on some craft-focused sites but not on jeweler sites, representing a missed opportunity for craft education.
The connection between body chemistry/environment and how an oxidized piece wears over time for an individual is only touched on by a few sources.
The specific aesthetic styles that heavily feature oxidized silver (e.g., Gothic, Bohemian, rustic, artisanal) are only briefly mentioned.
